structured.description: A `Shader` class that loads and manages shaders for OpenGL rendering. It includes functions for binding the shader program, uniform floating-point values, and matrix transformations. The `loadShader` method loads a vertex and fragment shader from files and creates a shader program. The `validateProgram` method validates the program and checks for errors.

items.description: from the file provides functions for binding and unbinding a program, as well as setting uniform values and matrices. The loadShader method loads vertex and fragment shaders into the program and validates the program after loading.
items.name: Shader

items.description: glues a program to be used by GL rendering functions.
items.name: bind

items.description: retrieves the value of a variable named `program`.

usage.code:
Shader myShader = Shader.loadShader("myShader");
int programId = myShader.getProgram();

items.name: getProgram

items.description: in Java takes a string parameter `name` and an array of floats `vals`, and uses the `GL20.glUniformf()` method to set a uniform float value for the specified location based on the length of the array.

params.name: name
params.description: location of the uniform in the program.

items.name: uniformf

items.description: sets a 4x4 uniform matrix using the `glUniformMatrix4` method, passing the matrix data as a flipped buffer to the GPU.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of the uniform location where the matrix will be stored.

params.name: mat
params.description:
4x4 homogeneous transformation matrix to be uniformed.

* The `Matrix4f` class is used to create a 4x4 homogeneous transformation matrix, which is the input to this function.
* The `getLinearData()` method returns the raw data of the matrix as a float array, which is then passed to `GL20.glUniformMatrix4()` for storage in the GPU's uniform buffer.
* The `Util.createFlippedBuffer()` method creates a flipped copy of the input matrix, which is necessary because the GPU's uniform buffer is only accessible as a left-handed matrix.

items.name: unifromMat4

items.description: retrieves the location of a uniform named `name` within a program's OpenGL 20 (GL20) handle.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the uniform location to retrieve in the OpenGL ES 2.0 program.

returns.description: an integer representing the location of a uniform in a program.

items.name: getLoaction

items.description: creates a shader program by loading and linking vertex and fragment shaders.

params.name: filename
params.description: name of the shader file to be loaded.

returns.description:
a new instance of the `Shader` class, representing a shader program created by combining a vertex shader and a fragment shader.

* `program`: An integer that represents the program object created by `glCreateProgram()`.
* `Shader`: A class instance that wraps the program object and provides methods for accessing its attributes.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: creates and compiles a shader program using the given source code, then attaches it to a program object.

params.name: target
params.description: type of shader (either Vertex or Fragment) that the function is loading.

params.name: src
params.description: source code of the shader that is to be compiled and linked into the program.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program into which the created shader is attached for use.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: validates a shader program by checking its linking and validation status, and prints any error messages if there are any.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program to be validated and linked with the GPU.

items.name: validateProgram

items.description: reads the contents of a given file as a string, handling potential IOExceptions and returning the resulting string.

params.name: file
params.description: path to a file that contains the text to be read.

returns.description: a string containing the contents of the specified file.

items.name: getText
